Alison Mackie.review_text: Professor Mackie has a clear, structured D2L that outlines week by week material to cover including lectures, topics, and assignments. Everything is manageable if you just follow her clear guide. On exams you're allowed a double-sided cheatsheet. Really great experience, it's a breath of fresh air to have a course so well organized!
easiest A of your life. do the homework, show up to class, and get a cheat sheet for the exams. like cmon, what else could you ask for. BABY FOOD

Lectures are quite long, and she tends to go through topics quickly, so it's easy to get lost if you're not paying attention. She's really helpful and allows for a lot of outside-of-class assistance. You are permitted a one-page cheat sheet and plenty of resources to help you study for the exams!
Lectures were slightly boring but that's accounting in general. Thought class was very fair and cheat sheet on exam was clutch. She was Very helpful with any questions you may have.

The lectures can be boring but she explains everything very simply and easy to understand. The study guides for the exams give you everything you need to know for the test. We also got a  1 page printed page of paper we could use on the test. She really wants you to succeed in the class and understand the principles of accounting.

Professor Mackie did a good job at explaining accounting concepts, and was easy to get in touch with if there was any questions or issues. I also enjoyed the good extra credit opportunities, and being able to review my exams outside of class.

Sherri Silverberg.review_text: Is she certified in Accounting? Yes. Does that mean she should be teaching it? No. She has proven she knows the tasks of an accountant but she is not much of a teacher/ educator. She jumps right into material as if it wasn't an introductory course. She works through examples quickly without explaining or teaching us. "Limited Term Adjunct Lecturer"

Sherri is one of the best teachers I have ever had. She inspired me to change my major to accounting. I think that most people exaggerate how hard this class is. If you put in effort and go to class you will do fine. Sherri really cares about her students and wants you to do the very best you can. She is also extremely nice and makes class fun!

You should buy the textbook and be prepared to teach yourself for the entire semester. She is willing to help. But willing to doesn't equal able to. She goes through many examples during classes. If you really enjoy learning from lectures, do not take her class.

Textbook is only a must because you will end up having to teach yourself if accounting is new to you. She is kind and is def certified in solving accounting problems really fast. But many of us are lost in class. The material is beneficial to know in "the real world" it just sucks that lectures are not very effective or helpful

This professor is a wonderful teacher who truly wanted to see everyone succeed. She gave multiple opportunities for extra credit and always provided quick feedback with lots of TA help as well. Study the problems she puts up and do the homework and quizzes, it's an easy A

Sherri is one of the best professors I've ever had. She emails you back within the same day, has many office hour times as well as weekly review sessions and even has office hours on the weekends before exams. What professor would do that? This course is not easy but if you pay attention in class, go to office hours, and study you'll be fine.

Sherri Silverberg is an outstanding professor. She is easily accessible via email, which is a huge plus. She is more than willing to help with any problem you have, and is one of the most encouraging professors I've ever had. If you need to take accounting, this professor will explain the most difficult concepts to you with ease. Highly recommend!

Sherri Is super nice, but ACCT 250 is a really hard class. Almost every student is completely lost and she doesn't curve exams even when the class average for exams are low 60%. She tries to be super helpful, but you are going to be teaching yourself all semester or at least trying to.

Sherri teaches a class that is going to be demanding &hard because it is the nature of the material. However, she has a willingness to help you anytime. She has been available on weekends &will meet you at a time that is convenient for you if her office hours don't work for you. She is so patient &kind and will do anything in her power to help you.

If you go to the lecture, pay 100% attention to it, because when she gives examples, there might be numbers suddenly comes out from nowhere. ***BUY/RENT THE TEXTBOOK!!! ***That's the MOST IMPORTANT thing you'll need for this class. You might have 90% chance end up teaching yourself throughout the semester.

Silverberg made me fall in love with accounting. Yes lectures can be a bit hard to get through at 2pm but that's any 2pm class. Getting an A is easy if you review the lecture slides and chapter before class then utilize all the practice sheets she provides. Exams are easy as long as you attended lectures and reviewed the chapters.

Professor Silverberg is a great professor for this course. She provides numerous opportunities outside of class to go over material with you if you dont understand it. Its obvious she really cares about her students passing, if you put in effort you will easily pass this course.

I have no idea what people are talking about... honestly. Might be one of the nicest professors I have ever met. The reviews on here are total slander! Shes given every single opportunity for students to ask questions, go over things, etc.

Her lectures were boring and not helpful as it was just reading off slides and she moved very quick so it's easy to fall behind if she's showing examples. Office hours helped me a lot, but if you don't understand something in the beginning units then you won't pass the class. The final is over all units over the semester so it was very challenging.

She is a nice professor and wants her students to do well, but accounting is by far the hardest class I've ever taken. She was vocal about office hours which helped me a lot, but during the lectures I found myself extremely lost as she didn't go into much detail. It was very boring and just reading off slides. Also, the final is cumulative.

Sherri was one of the best teachers I have had at the University of Arizona. Her lectures are very detailed and she explains accounting so well to make it easy to understand. Yes the class has some work, but if you just commit yourself to two days a week (lecture and homework days) you will be fine. She is a sweet and caring person.

I had this women for online classes this year because of covid-19. I thought I would do good, but the first test I totally failed. If you get her for ACCT250 just make sure you are able to access outside resources because learning online for this class was very difficult and she was not that helpful personally. Her tests are very important!!

Sherri was a really good professor for this class. The lectures are really long and there is a lot of information to understand but she is super accessible and super willing to help with any questions about anything. She is a super nice teacher and lays out everything that will be on the exams which is super nice so you know what to practice.

This class isn't that hard. Yes, there are a lot of moving parts in acct, but math is not difficult--it isn't even math! Print lecture slides, go to class (I can't emphasize it enough) & pay attention w/o having to take notes. When studying for exam(s), do ungraded problems on Wiley+ and do extra problem assignments and practice test(s) on D2L.

This class is as hard as it sounds. I thought it would be impossible but if you go to the lectures, see her at office hours and study study study!!!!!! you can do it! Lectures are usually boring and hard to understand because she goes so fast but she takes attendance and literally every point counts. She truly cares about her students though!

Sherri is the best. This class is hard but not impossible like some of the other reviews here say. All you have to do is study, go to class, and go to office hours where she'll help you with whatever you need. She truly cares and is rooting for all of her students. This class requires a lot of time/hard work but it doable. I am not good at math!

Sherri is the most caring teacher ever. She post everything online and gives you tons of extra problems to practice. The lectures are a little dry, but it isn't hard to teach yourself. She always emails back and is very encouraging. Just be prepared to put in some study hours or you won't pass.

Sherri is a great professor, but has become super strict about attendance. She is very knowledgeable and has good intentions, but gets tripped up a lot in lecture. Super accessible outside of class and emails back same day within hours. Not my favorite accounting professor I have had.

This class is a mission. Sherri is like this nice old lady, but her tests are rough and so is teachin. I went to office hours and did all the problems offered, and barely made a B on the first test. Did all the same for the second test and got a 54. Her class averages are anywhere fro a 55-65 on exams, it's pretty pathetic. The free tutoring sucks.

Kind person but very tough grader. The course is lecture heavy and she goes very fast and writes everything in illegible handwriting. Given the opportunity I suggest you take this class with a different professor.

Sherri is a good teacher. 250 is not easy, you have to put work in, and she knows it. She offers SO much help you'd be crazy not to use it. She answers email questions fast and seems to really care that you are learning. Happy to answers questions in class. You dont have to come, but miss class at your own risk. Super nice and sweet.

Sherri was honestly one of the best teachers I have had at the U of A. She is so kind and extremely willing to meet with you to answer any of your questions. I understand the class is challenging but if you keep up with it, it is possible to get an A. Do the pre-lecture quizzes and the activities before class. Print out her slides for class too.

Sherri is hands down the most caring and invested professor I've had at the U of A. Yes, she may go quickly during lecture but she'll slow down if you ask. She is a new professor and just has a lot to learn. She is willing to go out of her way for any student and will do anything to make sure you succeed in the class.

She sure is an accountant, but SHOULD NOT be teaching and INTRODUCTORY level accounting class designed for business MINORS. She goes extremely fast, hand writes the essential things you need to know with handwriting that you cannot read, and acts as if you already know the basics of accounting. DOES NOT prepare you for exams. DO NOT TAKE HER CLASS.

her teaching is absolutely terrible. You basically have to teach yourself cause if you don't get something she'll explain it maybe one extra time and then move on. Almost the whole class is confused 24/7. She gives you "opportunities for help" but when you go to accounting tutoring it's almost completely packed with act 210 students and 2 tutors.

Breanna Cicinelli.review_text: Bre is a super sweet professor. She is very knowledgable and has engaging lectures. She cares a lot about her students and makes herself available to help them. She is very organized and likes to keep her students on track with reminders. Accounting is tough, but she makes it more fun and easier to learn and shes very approachable.

Heather Altman.review_text: She's a very good professor. She teaches a very difficult class but that has nothing to do with her personal teaching style, Accounting is just difficult in general. She is willing to work with you outside of class to further explain concepts and she is extremely nice about it as well. Just be sure to actually read the textbook chapters, it helps

Being completely honest, you have to haul ass to pass this class. But it makes you feel great when you do. Mrs. Altman opened my eyes to accounting and personally I love it so much! She tries her best to get you to understand, and does not grade easily but honestly it makes you work harder. Recommend her for ACC 200/210 or 250. She's awesome!

Heather is a great instructor! Lectures are easy to follow. Mini pre-lecture quizzes every week and chapter activities you do alone or in a group at the end of class (quizzes and activities are done at home before bringing into class). Exams are over half your grade, so give yourself time to study. Attendance taken at random via your clicker.

Heather is a great prof. but this is a tough class. She goes through the material very quickly, leaving it up to you to really grasp the material by putting in the time outside of class. However, she makes herself and the preceptors very accessible outside of class. You'll have to work for your gra, but Heather makes this class a little bit easier.

Good and clear lecturer. She is passionate about the material, but moves through it a little slow. Class is not overly difficult, nothing but  formulas to remember, but the exams are difficult if you let the material get away from you.
Good teacher class in general is super boring.

Heather is the nicest professor I've had while at UA, she is very clear on what is expected to receive an A in this class. However if you want a good grade you have to study for the tests as they are over half of your course grade. She is also super helpful in office hours

Heather is one of the best teachers I've had, and she is so adorable.  Accounting is a tough class, but she makes it very easy to understand.  She goes over every assignment in class so you understand it and is always willing to answer questions. The tests are difficult but definitely manageable if you study and go over the assignments beforehand.

Sure this class is a lot of work; there is something to turn in most every day. Even though Heather's lectures don't exactly make Acct more interesting, they are SO clear in explaining the material (way better than the textbook). You'll need the book for the HW though. The practice exams she gives are super helpful. Would recommend her

amazing professor, she gives a practice exam that gives you a really good idea what the exam are going to be like. class is kinda mandatory, she gives clicker quizzes once a week and group activities once a week but they are easy points. TA is awesome too. favorite class!

Heather is by far one of the best professors I have ever had. Beyond being approachable and sweet, she explains the material in the most helpful way possible and is clear about expectations. Assignmentsquizzes make attendance importance but her lectures are enjoyable and go by fast. She provides practice tests, very similar to exams. Love her!

I had this women for online classes this year because of covid-19. I thought I would do good, but the first test I totally failed. If you get her for ACCT250 just make sure you are able to access outside resources because learning online for this class was very difficult and she was not that helpful personally. Her tests are very important!!

Sherri was a really good professor for this class. The lectures are really long and there is a lot of information to understand but she is super accessible and super willing to help with any questions about anything. She is a super nice teacher and lays out everything that will be on the exams which is super nice so you know what to practice.

This class isn't that hard. Yes, there are a lot of moving parts in acct, but math is not difficult--it isn't even math! Print lecture slides, go to class (I can't emphasize it enough) & pay attention w/o having to take notes. When studying for exam(s), do ungraded problems on Wiley+ and do extra problem assignments and practice test(s) on D2L.

This class is as hard as it sounds. I thought it would be impossible but if you go to the lectures, see her at office hours and study study study!!!!!! you can do it! Lectures are usually boring and hard to understand because she goes so fast but she takes attendance and literally every point counts. She truly cares about her students though!

Sherri is the best. This class is hard but not impossible like some of the other reviews here say. All you have to do is study, go to class, and go to office hours where she'll help you with whatever you need. She truly cares and is rooting for all of her students. This class requires a lot of time/hard work but it doable. I am not good at math!

Sherri is the most caring teacher ever. She post everything online and gives you tons of extra problems to practice. The lectures are a little dry, but it isn't hard to teach yourself. She always emails back and is very encouraging. Just be prepared to put in some study hours or you won't pass.

Sherri is a great professor, but has become super strict about attendance. She is very knowledgeable and has good intentions, but gets tripped up a lot in lecture. Super accessible outside of class and emails back same day within hours. Not my favorite accounting professor I have had.

This class is a mission. Sherri is like this nice old lady, but her tests are rough and so is teachin. I went to office hours and did all the problems offered, and barely made a B on the first test. Did all the same for the second test and got a 54. Her class averages are anywhere fro a 55-65 on exams, it's pretty pathetic. The free tutoring sucks.

Kind person but very tough grader. The course is lecture heavy and she goes very fast and writes everything in illegible handwriting. Given the opportunity I suggest you take this class with a different professor.

Sherri is a good teacher. 250 is not easy, you have to put work in, and she knows it. She offers SO much help you'd be crazy not to use it. She answers email questions fast and seems to really care that you are learning. Happy to answers questions in class. You dont have to come, but miss class at your own risk. Super nice and sweet.

Sherri was honestly one of the best teachers I have had at the U of A. She is so kind and extremely willing to meet with you to answer any of your questions. I understand the class is challenging but if you keep up with it, it is possible to get an A. Do the pre-lecture quizzes and the activities before class. Print out her slides for class too.

Sherri is hands down the most caring and invested professor I've had at the U of A. Yes, she may go quickly during lecture but she'll slow down if you ask. She is a new professor and just has a lot to learn. She is willing to go out of her way for any student and will do anything to make sure you succeed in the class.

She sure is an accountant, but SHOULD NOT be teaching and INTRODUCTORY level accounting class designed for business MINORS. She goes extremely fast, hand writes the essential things you need to know with handwriting that you cannot read, and acts as if you already know the basics of accounting. DOES NOT prepare you for exams. DO NOT TAKE HER CLASS.

her teaching is absolutely terrible. You basically have to teach yourself cause if you don't get something she'll explain it maybe one extra time and then move on. Almost the whole class is confused 24/7. She gives you "opportunities for help" but when you go to accounting tutoring it's almost completely packed with act 210 students and 2 tutors.

Bre is a super sweet professor. She is very knowledgable and has engaging lectures. She cares a lot about her students and makes herself available to help them. She is very organized and likes to keep her students on track with reminders. Accounting is tough, but she makes it more fun and easier to learn and shes very approachable.

She's a very good professor. She teaches a very difficult class but that has nothing to do with her personal teaching style, Accounting is just difficult in general. She is willing to work with you outside of class to further explain concepts and she is extremely nice about it as well. Just be sure to actually read the textbook chapters, it helps

Being completely honest, you have to haul ass to pass this class. But it makes you feel great when you do. Mrs. Altman opened my eyes to accounting and personally I love it so much! She tries her best to get you to understand, and does not grade easily but honestly it makes you work harder. Recommend her for ACC 200/210 or 250. She's awesome!

Exams are over half your grade, so give yourself time to study. Attendance taken at random via your clicker.

Good teacher class in general is super boring.

Heather is the nicest professor I've had while at UA, she is very clear on what is expected to receive an A in this class. However if you want a good grade you have to study for the tests as they are over half of your course grade. She is also super helpful in office hours

Heather is one of the best teachers I've had, and she is so adorable.  Accounting is a tough class, but she makes it very easy to understand.  She goes over every assignment in class so you understand it and is always willing to answer questions. The tests are difficult but definitely manageable if you study and go over the assignments beforehand.

The practice exams she gives are super helpful. Would recommend her

amazing professor, she gives a practice exam that gives you a really good idea what the exam are going to be like. class is kinda mandatory, she gives clicker quizzes once a week and group activities once a week but they are easy points. TA is awesome too. favorite class!

Heather is by far one of the best professors I have ever had. Beyond being approachable and sweet, she explains the material in the most helpful way possible and is clear about expectations. Assignmentsquizzes make attendance importance but her lectures are enjoyable and go by fast. She provides practice tests, very similar to exams. Love her!
